race.name: L'Etape Chile by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Etape Chile by Tour de France
race.tagline: 110km of Chilean coastal fury with 1,200m punch—Tour de France soul hits the Southern Hemisphere!

vitals.registration: Opens early 2026 via official site; expect €150-250 including finisher's jersey, support, and Tour prestige
vitals.feed_zones: 6-8 fully stocked with gels, bars, hydration, and Chilean empanadas every 15-20km
vitals.cutoff_time: 8-10 hours for masses; broom wagon mercy

climate.description: November brings mild 15-22°C days with high humidity from Valdivia's rivers and coast. Expect occasional drizzle turning roads slick but refreshing the soul. Sunny breaks highlight Andean foothills in emerald glory.

terrain.primary: Coastal rollers through lush Valdivian rainforest
terrain.surface: Smooth asphalt with occasional chipseal; riverside paths wind through ancient alerce forests. Fresh pavement expected for debut, but watch for wet leaves and gravel shoulders. Pure Southern Cone poetry on two wheels.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 63. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 61.

course_description.character: Coastal launch from Niebla rockets into Valdivia's emerald valleys. Rollers twist through ancient forests, rivers flashing beside. Gentle climbs build to panoramic payoffs—no endless grinds, all scenic reward. TdF signage elevates every pedal.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Niebla-to-Valdivia coastal traverse: 110km essence in rainforest-wrapped rollers. 1,200m spread thin but relentless, with humidity amplifying burn. TdF finish line in Valdivia seals the conquest.

logistics.airport: Fly into Valdivia (ZOS) or connect via Santiago (SCL, 1hr flight). Shuttles from Niebla start; taxis cheap.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Book Valdivia hotels/B&Bs early—riverside gems abound. Event camps likely. Avoid peak summer surge.
logistics.food: Empanadas, fresh seafood, craft beer explosion. Vegan options growing. Post-race asados legendary.

history.origin_story: Born from TdF's global conquest, L'Etape Chile plants the yellow jersey in Patagonia. First Southern Cone edition targets nature-loving amateurs. Valdivia chosen for epic coast-river fusion. Debut promises closed roads and pro vibes.
history.reputation: Tour pedigree ensures quality. Chile's debut builds hype. Early buzz solid from series fans.

biased_opinion.summary: L'Etape Chile blasts Tour spirit into Chile's lush south, blending coastal beauty with manageable suffering. Debut 110km from Niebla immerses you in rainforest rollers under Tour branding—no French Alps brutality, but fresh adventure. Skeptics yawn at low vert; purists drool over novelty. Valdivia's charm post-ride rivals any podium.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Signature entry for Southern Hemisphere TdF dreams. Nail training, dodge rain—claim your jersey. World needs more events like this.

final_verdict.one_liner: TdF magic meets Chilean wilderness—worth the trek!
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if craving exotic road prestige without Alpine agony. Skip if chasing mega-vert or elite competition.
final_verdict.alternatives: Unreal Gran Fondo (unreal-granfondo), L'Etape Costa Rica (letape-costa-rica), TransAndes Challenge (transandes)
